assignment_description:
Model 1 Envelope
⍞ Materials: Paper Sloyd for Primary Grades, cardstock, ruler, pencil, scissors, bone, awl, glue stick

➜ NOTE
Teachers need to watch Sloyd Introduction before the first lesson and complete the reading assignment in order to understand the purpose and process of Sloyd.
∞ Video Link: Sloyd Introduction

➜ VIEW
Photo of the model and tutorial.
∞ Image & Video Link: Envelope

➜ PRACTICE
Work on the model together. Read and demonstrate instructions one step at a time.
First Year Models "I. Envelope" p.9
Use the envelope to send a letter to a friend or family member. Reinforce the seams with tape so it holds up in the mail.

∞ Image & Video Link: Tray

➜ PRACTICE

First Year Models "VI. Tray" p.14
Make several of these, and glue them together on the outsides to make a drawer organizer. They work great to hold little things like thumbtacks, paper clips, erasers, etc.

Model 10 Fan
⍞ Materials: Paper Sloyd for Primary Grades, tagboard, ruler, pencil, scissors, glue, awl or hole punch, tape, string

➜ VIEW
Photo of the model and tutorial.
∞ Image & Video Link: Fan

➜ PRACTICE
Work on the model together. Read and demonstrate instructions one step at a time.
First Year Models "X. Fan" p.19
Making a paper fan is an essential skill. You never know when you are going to need it, but it is useful when you do. Fans can also be used as party decor. Attaching several of them end-to-end will make an accordion circle, which is great for hanging or wall decor.
